name: Stranded on a Tropical Island with the Popular Quiet Girl
description:
You're stranded on a lovely tropical island with the popular but quiet girl from school!

Your end of year school vacation is cut short when the plane carrying your class encounters turbulence and crashes onto a small tropical island in the middle of nowhere. However, only you and Layla wake up on the beach, everyone else is gone. No bodies, no blood, nothing, just poof.

The island itself is paradise, almost too perfect - definitely too perfect. Crops and fruit grow back quick, the trees are perfect for chopping down and making shelter, the water is clean, there's a mysterious metal ore, and one very sexy, worried, lonely lady.

Bot definitions are open, specific information about the bot is there.

This is my second ever bot made from another idea I've been day dreaming about for months. I tried a different approach with the definitions but kept it pretty basic and vague to let everyone essentially craft their own survival story, especially with the hinted mysterious aspect of the island.

Layla herself is more a fluff bot naturally with how her personality is presented, however given the scenario and reading her personality it's very easy to turn her smutty.

There are no threats or other people on the island per the definitions so the bot should never introduce people by itself. However if you want other people or threats, in my testing I've managed to force past that part of the definition by just introducing a situation where my character discovers people on the island somehow themselves and it worked perfectly fine.

I tested this bot with the new Kimi 2.5 Thinking andddd personally I find it's absolutely amazing, with a good prompt that is. Highly recommend.

Here's a bunch of pictures I generated of Layla, I'm still getting used to creating and finding my way with generating character images so they're quite basic. But she's pretty.

Change Log:

2/5/25 - Removed forced second person present tense prose rule from prompt. (Initial message is the same but this will allow everyone to override the prose style if they want with OOC)

1/31/25 - Published, removed info bar.
creator: TimeRupture
